message:
fix: mobile responsive layout, search UX, and multiple bug fixes

Mobile responsive improvements:
- Add explicit width: 100% to container utility classes (.container-narrow,
  .container-default, .container-wide) so they fill their parent on small
  screens instead of collapsing to content width
- Export Next.js Viewport config with device-width, user-scalable, and
  maximumScale=5 for accessibility compliance
- Scale hero headline from text-3xl on mobile up to text-7xl on desktop,
  and remove fixed max-width constraints at small breakpoints so text and
  search input use full available width
- Remove min-w-max on mobile category filter pills so they wrap instead
  of forcing horizontal scroll

Search & UX fixes:
- Show skeleton loading state during the 150ms debounce window (not just
  during semantic reranking) so users see immediate feedback when typing
- Fix "no results" message appearing prematurely by gating it on
  !isSearching && !isReranking
- Fix Button component: when loading=true, fall back from Radix Slot to
  native <button> since Slot requires exactly one child and the loading
  spinner fragment breaks that contract

Bug fixes:
- useLocalStorage: keep initialValueRef.current in sync with the
  initialValue prop so re-renders with a new default don't use the stale
  first-render value
- Transcript processor: track gap count separately from section length in
  edit-section detection, and rewind endIdx to trim trailing gap messages
  from the detected section boundary
- CLI serve: capture total match count before slicing to `limit` so the
  API response reports the true total, not the truncated page size; also
  guard render_prompt against non-object variable inputs
- Registry: fix "AGENTS md" typo → "AGENTS.md" in prompt content
- BM25: prevent NaN from 0/0 division when prompts array is empty by
  using Math.max(prompts.length, 1) in avgDocLength calculation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

     difficulty: "intermediate",
     estimatedTokens: 400,
     created: "2025-09-17",
-    content: `I want you to sort of randomly explore the code files in this project, choosing code files to deeply investigate and understand and trace their functionality and execution flows through the related code files which they import or which they are imported by. Once you understand the purpose of the code in the larger context of the workflows, I want you to do a super careful, methodical, and critical check with "fresh eyes" to find any obvious bugs, problems, errors, issues, silly mistakes, etc. and then systematically and meticulously and intelligently correct them. Be sure to comply with ALL rules in the AGENTS md file and ensure that any code you write or revise conforms to the best practice guides referenced in the AGENTS md file.`,
+    content: `I want you to sort of randomly explore the code files in this project, choosing code files to deeply investigate and understand and trace their functionality and execution flows through the related code files which they import or which they are imported by. Once you understand the purpose of the code in the larger context of the workflows, I want you to do a super careful, methodical, and critical check with "fresh eyes" to find any obvious bugs, problems, errors, issues, silly mistakes, etc. and then systematically and meticulously and intelligently correct them. Be sure to comply with ALL rules in the AGENTS.md file and ensure that any code you write or revise conforms to the best practice guides referenced in the AGENTS.md file.`,
     whenToUse: [
       "After writing a lot of new code",
       "When you suspect there might be bugs lurking",
